$description: Canvas data-visualization tokens. Semantic names for trace colors, backgrounds, grid, guidelines, and text used inside Canvas-rendered visualizations (Lissajous, waveform, harmonic spectrum, resolution comparison, etc). All references resolve to base palette tokens so Canvas consumers stay in sync with CSS/Tailwind consumers. Rationale: shipped Canvas components on feat/visual-signature-components each re-invented their own COLORS object with slight drift (#0fc8c3 vs #0d9e99); graduating these into tokens removes the drift and lets every consumer — www React, Remotion, Hyperframes agents — read from one source.

primary.$description: Default Canvas trace color. Healthy signal, normal signature, primary waveform.

fault.$description: Fault / critical / alert state. Bearing fault Lissajous, fault-severity bars, critical degradation arrows.

warning.$description: Elevated / early-warning state. H5 downward arrow, below-alarm-threshold indicators.

dark.$description: Canvas background on dark surfaces. Oscilloscope aesthetic.

light.$description: Canvas background on light surfaces. Technical-illustration aesthetic.

grid.$description: Center crosshair, minor gridlines, bar outlines on dark Canvas surfaces.

dark.$description: Near-invisible reference geometry (unit circles, outer bounds) on dark Canvas surfaces. Explicit rgba — this is the one place alpha-on-white is allowed, for sub-pixel Canvas guidelines.

light.$description: Near-invisible reference geometry on light Canvas surfaces.

label.$description: Primary axis labels, panel headings inside Canvas (RAW ELECTRICAL SIGNAL, HEALTH ANALYSIS). JetBrains Mono, 8px.

meta.$description: Secondary metadata inside Canvas (power, PF, status text). JetBrains Mono, 7–9px.
